full_text:
The TME is organized in spatial structures

Jia-Ren Lin, 2023

Some structures can be observed only if we sample them with enough antibodies, resolution, or sample size

Histopathology using whole slides tissue sections has been a gold standard in oncology

Pro:
Large section of tissue. 


Cons:
Large amount of data

Tissue microarrays (TMAs) are a technique to study small tissue sections from several samples in parallel on the same slide

Paraffin block

Slide

For high-plex imagining of cohorts, much less data is created with TMAs
Easier to analyze and identify common patterns (biomarkers) in cohorts

Lin et al., 2023, Cell

Paraffin block

Slide

Can TMAs capture highly dissimilar features of the TME among clinical-molecular groups in HGSC?

DAPI CK7 ECadherin CD11c CD8 αSMA

Before chemo

After chemo

*Neoadjuvant chemotherapy, platinum based and paclitaxel

Stromal rich

Tumor area

Myeloid rich

The study questions

Can we use TMA sampling to develop biomarkers using cohorts?
Is the intra-sample variability smaller than inter-sample variability?

Is there an strategy for TMA construction that increase concordance to the whole slides?
Guided by pathologist instead of random?
How many cores needed?

Preliminarily approach done in summer internship in
Peter Sorger’s Lab in 2023

Study materials

There 22 tCycif samples from the sciset project

1. Core selection guided by Human

Study approach

-[o] Non-overlapping = True 
-[r]Core radius = 500µm
-[n] N=4

Conclusions till here

In HGSC, TMAs guided by human are able to recapture similar cell-proportions as the whole slide images (WSI)

Random TMAs had less concordance with WSI

Guided TMAs can recapture short distance cell-cell neighborhoods comparable to WSI

Conclusions till here

In HGSC, TMAs guided by human are able to recapture similar cell-proportions as the whole slide images (WSI)

Random TMAs had less concordance with WSI

Guided TMAs can recapture short distance cell-cell neighborhoods comparable to WSI

Next steps

Multiple parameter evaluations to created the guided and random TMAs in 22 whole slide images (by Antti Toivanen)

Selection of best parameters (core radius, number of cores) 

Evaluation of concordance to previously detected biological findings in Cancer cell paper

Next step: Evaluate core selection from H&E with matched tCycIF

Core selection by Pathologist in H&E (Anni Virtanen)

4 cores of each

In total 12 cores

3 types of cores

iCAN-DIP - Lassi Paavolainen and Naipunya Guruprasad

i. At the pixel level – patches – will the model learn enough from TMA vs whole slide? 
ii. What associations can be made with other data, e.g., genomic data – to be excluded.
